findings.summary: No committed test for file-present-but-uncomputable statistic (null/NaN/bool/missing-directional pearson_ic); reviewer empirically verified all four variants fail closed as ERROR with DetectionStatisticError. Suggest follow-up fixture test.

Detection threshold declared twice (canary fixture JSON 0.95 and TRUE_ALPHA_DETECTION_THRESHOLD_ABS_PEARSON_IC constant); drift is CI-pinned by test_detection_fixtures_declare_threshold_pair asserting fixture == constant.
reason_code UNDERPOWERED reused for expected statistic non-pass on null runs; pre-existing enum reuse, semantically imprecise but harmless.
gate_outcome records threshold and diagnostic name but not the measured IC, consistent with value-free artifact policy; intentional.

mutation_tests.mutation: detection_statistic.py: detected = measured >= threshold -> measured < threshold
mutation_tests.detail: 16 failures including BOTH P040500 canary tests (strong detected, weak not detected) AND surrogate statistic/calibration tests; proves one shared IC truth consumed by both legs.
4 failures: statistic-pass test, LEAKAGE_BLOCKED calibration test, statistic-over-eligibility test, P05 zero-pass canary; zero-pass not vacuous.

mutation_tests.mutation: run_real_surrogate_calibration.py: rescore drops ERROR rows (silent missing-seed skip)
